description:
A wind energy research engineer is building a parametric blade geometry model for a small horizontal-axis wind turbine. The blade uses the S809 airfoil at the root and the S805A airfoil at the tip. 

Your task:
1. Read the blade geometry specification from `/home/ga/Desktop/blade_spec.txt`.
2. Open OpenVSP and create a new Wing geometry component.
3. Modify the root cross-section to use an 'AF File' (Airfoil File) instead of the default NACA shape, and load `/home/ga/Desktop/s809.dat`.
4. Modify the tip cross-section to also use an 'AF File' and load `/home/ga/Desktop/s805a.dat`.
5. Set the wing planform parameters according to the specification document (Total Span: 5.0m, Root Chord: 0.80m, Tip Chord: 0.30m, Sweep: 0, Dihedral: 0).
6. Save the completed model to `/home/ga/Documents/OpenVSP/turbine_blade.vsp3`.
